title: Eliminate MinIO; add SeaweedFS + RustFS as S3-compatible object-store chooser
description: MinIO discontinued community-edition support. Replace it entirely with two S3-compatible alternatives selectable via a helm/workload chooser: SeaweedFS (default, mature, scale-proven) and RustFS (opt-in, MinIO-API-compatible drop-in). The actual code path stays as the S3 backend pointing at a custom endpoint (`NOETL_S3_ENDPOINT`) — both alternatives expose S3-compatible APIs. What changes is deployment topology (manifests + helm + infrastructure playbooks), ConfigMap endpoint values, and docstrings/comments referencing MinIO. Discovery-first round: phase 1 inventories every MinIO touchpoint; subsequent phases replace by category. Cap: up to 3 PRs (1 ops, 1 noetl, 1 docs). Connects to the AMBER storage-tier finding from round 20260511-110000 — once GKE re-deploys, that round's GUI gap follow-up is the only Path A leftover, and storage Path B may resolve as a side effect.

design_brief_for_codex.inventory_baseline:
Claude pre-grepped MinIO references — these are the known touchpoints (Codex should verify completeness in phase 1):

  repos/ops:
    - automation/helm/noetl/values.yaml
    - automation/setup/bootstrap.yaml
    - automation/setup/destroy.yaml
    - automation/infrastructure/minio.yaml
    - ci/kind/config.yaml
    - ci/manifests/noetl/configmap-worker.yaml
    - ci/manifests/noetl/configmap-server.yaml
    - ci/manifests/minio/{namespace,persistent-volume,deployment,service}.yaml

  repos/noetl:
    - noetl/core/storage/router.py            (comments + StoreTier docstrings — code stays)
    - noetl/core/storage/backends.py          (comments + S3 backend docstring — code stays)
    - noetl/core/storage/result_store.py
    - noetl/core/storage/models.py
    - noetl/core/storage/__init__.py
    - noetl/core/dsl/engine/models/tools.py
    - noetl/tools/artifact/executor.py
    - noetl/core/config.py
    - noetl/database/ddl/postgres/schema_ddl.sql
    - noetl/core/dsl/playbook.schema.json
    - ci/manifests/noetl/configmap-{worker,server}.yaml
design_brief_for_codex.design_decision_chooser: Helm + bootstrap playbook accept `objectStore.kind: seaweedfs | rustfs`. Default: `seaweedfs` for maturity. Both produce a Service named `object-store` (or similar canonical name) in the configured namespace exposing the S3-compatible API on port 9000 (S3 standard) or per backend convention. Workers + server's `NOETL_S3_ENDPOINT` env var resolves to `http://object-store.<ns>.svc.cluster.local:<port>` regardless of which backend is deployed. From the worker's perspective, nothing changes — same boto3 client, same custom endpoint, same bucket conventions.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_1_inventory_and_design: Codex re-greps to confirm Claude's inventory is complete (add any missing files). Pick the canonical Service name + port to expose both backends behind (recommendation: `object-store.<ns>.svc:9000`, mirroring the S3 default). Decide bucket naming convention preserved from MinIO (default bucket `noetl`).
design_brief_for_codex.phase_2_new_manifests:
Create:
  - `repos/ops/ci/manifests/seaweedfs/` — namespace, persistent-volume, deployment (filer + S3 gateway minimum), service exposing port 8333 for S3 (SeaweedFS convention) OR port 9000 if remapped via the service. Mirror the directory + file structure of the existing `minio/` manifests.
  - `repos/ops/ci/manifests/rustfs/` — namespace, persistent-volume, deployment, service. RustFS exposes port 9000 by default (MinIO-compatible).
  - Both expose the same `object-store` Service name so consumers don't have to differentiate.
  - Image references: SeaweedFS official `chrislusf/seaweedfs:3.x` (latest stable), RustFS official `rustfs/rustfs:latest`.
  - Persistent volume conventions match the existing MinIO PV shape (size, storage class, hostPath for kind).
design_brief_for_codex.phase_3_infrastructure_playbooks:
Create `repos/ops/automation/infrastructure/object_store.yaml` that replaces `automation/infrastructure/minio.yaml`. Takes a workload field `objectstore_kind: seaweedfs | rustfs` and dispatches to the matching manifest set. Old `minio.yaml` is DELETED (not deprecated — eliminate completely per user requirement).

Update `automation/setup/bootstrap.yaml`: replace MinIO setup steps with object-store-chooser setup. Pass `objectstore` choice through.

Update `automation/setup/destroy.yaml`: clean both seaweedfs and rustfs namespaces (whichever was used). Drop MinIO cleanup.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_4_helm_chart_chooser:
`repos/ops/automation/helm/noetl/values.yaml` gets:
  ```yaml
  objectStore:
    kind: seaweedfs        # seaweedfs | rustfs
    namespace: noetl-storage
    image:
      seaweedfs: chrislusf/seaweedfs:3.x
      rustfs: rustfs/rustfs:latest
    storage:
      size: 50Gi
      storageClass: ''      # empty → default StorageClass
    bucket: noetl
    credentials:
      accessKey: noetl-access
      secretKey: <managed>
  ```
  Drop the existing `minio:` block entirely. Update any helm templates that referenced minio to read from `objectStore.*` instead. Add helm conditionals to deploy the right template set based on `objectStore.kind`.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_5_configmap_and_endpoint_wiring:
`repos/ops/ci/manifests/noetl/configmap-{worker,server}.yaml` + `repos/noetl/ci/manifests/noetl/configmap-{worker,server}.yaml`:
  - `NOETL_S3_ENDPOINT` env var points at the canonical `http://object-store.<ns>.svc.cluster.local:<port>` regardless of backend.
  - `NOETL_S3_ACCESS_KEY` + `NOETL_S3_SECRET_KEY` either match the new backend's credentials or are sourced from a Kubernetes Secret named consistently.
  - Remove any MinIO-specific env vars (`MINIO_ROOT_USER`, etc.) — those were only for the MinIO deployment, not for noetl clients.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_6_noetl_code_comment_sweep:
In `repos/noetl/noetl/core/storage/`:
  - `router.py` lines 13, 31, 32, 38, 39 mention MinIO. Replace with 'S3-compatible (SeaweedFS, RustFS, AWS S3, etc.)'. Keep `StoreTier.S3` as the enum value — the tier is abstract.
  - `backends.py` line 10 and the `"""S3/MinIO storage for large objects."""` docstring at line 629 same treatment.
  - Look for any test fixtures referencing MinIO endpoints — update to point at object-store.
  - `noetl/database/ddl/postgres/schema_ddl.sql` + `noetl/core/dsl/playbook.schema.json` + `noetl/core/config.py` + `noetl/tools/artifact/executor.py` + the rest of the noetl grep hits — read each, decide whether to remove the MinIO reference or generalize to 'S3-compatible'. Do NOT remove functionality; only adjust naming.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_7_docs_sweep: `repos/docs/`: grep for minio/MinIO/min.io references. Update operations docs, infrastructure guides, and any tutorial that mentions MinIO to reference the new chooser. Likely few hits but worth confirming. Reference the chooser pattern + which backend is the default.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_8_smoke_kind:
Re-bootstrap a fresh kind cluster with `objectstore=seaweedfs` (default). Verify:
  1. SeaweedFS pod healthy in the storage namespace.
  2. NOETL_S3_ENDPOINT resolves to the Service inside noetl-server / noetl-worker pods.
  3. Submit a noetl execution with a large-payload step that spills to the S3 tier (e.g. an Amadeus activities call via the travel agent now that the catalog is on session-latest). Verify the result is durable across worker pod restart (kill the worker, restart, check the cached result is still retrievable from the storage tier — that's the actual MinIO replacement test).
  4. Repeat the bootstrap with `objectstore=rustfs`. Same smokes. Confirm the chooser works.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_9_smoke_gke: Re-deploy on GKE with the new chooser. The GKE deployment currently uses S3 tier per round 20260511-110000 finding — that S3 might be a remote bucket OR was a MinIO endpoint. Identify which. If MinIO: replace with seaweedfs (default). If a remote S3 (AWS): keep that as a separate cloud-tier option — the chooser is for in-cluster, AWS S3 is a different deployment model. Document the distinction.
design_brief_for_codex.phase_10_close_out: Memory entry, sync issue closure for Path B (storage tier finding), validation log paragraph, ai-meta pointer bumps for ops + noetl + docs.

1_inventory_and_design_pick.objective: Verify Claude's MinIO inventory is complete; finalize the canonical Service/port + chooser shape.
spec: Re-grep MinIO/minio/min.io across repos/ops, repos/noetl, repos/docs, repos/gui, repos/e2e. Add any files Claude missed.
Finalize: canonical Service name (`object-store` recommended), port (9000 standard), bucket name (`noetl`).
Document the chooser values: `objectStore.kind: seaweedfs | rustfs` with default `seaweedfs`.
Pick image tags (SeaweedFS stable, RustFS stable).
1_inventory_and_design_pick.verify: Inventory + design recorded in result file.

spec: Mirror the structure of existing ci/manifests/minio/ (namespace, persistent-volume, deployment, service).
Both Services use the canonical name + port from phase 1.
Persistent volume sizes/storage classes match MinIO's existing shape so disk allocation is comparable.
2_new_manifests.verify: kubectl apply --dry-run=client on each manifest set succeeds.

3_infrastructure_playbooks.objective: Replace minio infrastructure playbook with object_store chooser; update bootstrap/destroy.
spec: Create automation/infrastructure/object_store.yaml taking `objectstore_kind` workload field.
DELETE automation/infrastructure/minio.yaml.
Update automation/setup/bootstrap.yaml to use the new chooser playbook + pass through the kind choice.
Update automation/setup/destroy.yaml to clean up whichever backend was deployed.
Pydantic-validate the new playbooks.
3_infrastructure_playbooks.verify: New playbook validates; bootstrap + destroy work end-to-end in dry-run.

4_helm_chart_chooser.objective: Helm chart values + templates support the objectStore chooser.
spec: Update repos/ops/automation/helm/noetl/values.yaml — drop the minio block, add objectStore block per the design brief.
Update helm templates to read objectStore.* and conditionally deploy SeaweedFS or RustFS resources.
Drop minio-specific templates entirely.
Run `helm template` on both `kind=seaweedfs` and `kind=rustfs` to confirm rendering succeeds.
4_helm_chart_chooser.verify: helm template renders cleanly for both backends.

5_configmap_and_endpoint_wiring.objective: NOETL_S3_ENDPOINT + credentials env vars point at the canonical Service in both ConfigMaps.
spec: Update repos/ops/ci/manifests/noetl/configmap-{worker,server}.yaml.
Update repos/noetl/ci/manifests/noetl/configmap-{worker,server}.yaml.
Drop MINIO_ROOT_USER / MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD if present.
Verify the new endpoint URL resolves from inside noetl-server / noetl-worker pods (use a quick kubectl exec to confirm DNS + reachability after deploy).
5_configmap_and_endpoint_wiring.verify: Endpoint URL string is correct in both ConfigMaps; backend-agnostic.

6_noetl_code_comment_sweep.objective: Replace MinIO references in noetl code comments + docstrings + schema. Keep functionality unchanged.
spec: Update noetl/core/storage/{router,backends,result_store,models,__init__}.py — replace 'S3/MinIO' / 'MinIO' references with 'S3-compatible (SeaweedFS, RustFS, AWS S3)'.
Update noetl/core/config.py, noetl/tools/artifact/executor.py, noetl/core/dsl/engine/models/tools.py.
Update noetl/database/ddl/postgres/schema_ddl.sql + noetl/core/dsl/playbook.schema.json — remove or generalize MinIO references.
Run existing tests: `pytest tests/ -k 'storage or backend or router'` — all must pass.
6_noetl_code_comment_sweep.verify: MinIO references gone from noetl code comments. Existing tests still pass.

7_docs_sweep.objective: Update repos/docs to reflect the new chooser.
spec: grep -r -i 'minio' repos/docs — fix every hit.
Update operations / infrastructure docs to describe the objectStore chooser.
If there's an architecture doc explicitly covering the storage tier, update it to mention SeaweedFS + RustFS as backends.
`npm run build` clean in repos/docs.
7_docs_sweep.verify: Docs build clean. No MinIO references remain.

8_smoke_kind.objective: Re-bootstrap fresh kind with each backend; verify storage spillover works across pod restart.
spec: Tear down current kind, bootstrap fresh with `objectstore=seaweedfs`.
Verify SeaweedFS pod healthy.
Trigger an execution that spills to the S3 tier (a long activities response, or a synthetic large-payload artifact).
Kill the noetl-worker pod, wait for restart, verify the cached result is still retrievable from the storage tier (this is the durability test that worker-local disk fails).
Repeat the bootstrap with `objectstore=rustfs`. Same smokes.
Document any RustFS-specific behavior differences (its MinIO-API-compat is supposed to be drop-in; flag any deviations).
8_smoke_kind.verify: Both backends pass the durability test on kind.

9_smoke_gke.objective: Deploy the new chooser on GKE and verify.
spec: Inspect what S3 endpoint GKE currently uses — was it MinIO in-cluster or a remote bucket? Document.
If MinIO in-cluster: replace with SeaweedFS (default) via the chooser.
If remote AWS S3: leave that path alone (it's a different deployment model, not the in-cluster object store). Document the distinction in the round notes.
Smoke the travel agent activities path again on GKE — confirm storage tier behavior matches kind.
Capture image tags + pod state.
9_smoke_gke.verify: GKE on new chooser (or documented as remote-S3 keeping current setup).

10_open_and_merge_prs.objective: Up to 3 PRs (ops, noetl, docs). All squash-merged.
spec: ops PR: `feat(infra): replace MinIO with SeaweedFS + RustFS object-store chooser`. Includes manifests, helm, playbooks, configmaps.
noetl PR: `chore(storage): replace MinIO references with S3-compatible backend naming`. Code comments + docstrings + schema.
docs PR: `docs(infra): document SeaweedFS + RustFS object-store chooser, remove MinIO`.
Sequencing: ops PR FIRST (deploy artifacts), then noetl + docs (can land in parallel).

11_close_out_and_pointer_bumps.objective: Sync issue closure, validation log, memory entry, submodule pointer bumps in ai-meta.
spec: Close the Path B section of sync/issues/2026-05-11-gke-deployment-drift-and-storage-tier-audit.md — note the chooser is now in place; the cloud-tier-router question (GCS vs S3) is a separate decision still open.
Append paragraph to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md.
Memory entry naming the chooser default + deploy state on kind + GKE.
`git submodule update --remote repos/ops repos/noetl repos/docs && git add ... && git commit -m 'chore(sync): bump ops/noetl/docs for MinIO→SeaweedFS/RustFS chooser'`. Don't push ai-meta.
11_close_out_and_pointer_bumps.verify: ai-meta has a fresh local commit covering the three submodule bumps.

expected_state.description: MinIO is completely eliminated from all repos. The deployment model offers a chooser between SeaweedFS (default) and RustFS, both exposing S3-compatible APIs at the canonical `object-store` Service. noetl code references the abstract S3 backend; comments updated. Both kind and GKE work with the new chooser.
expected_state.verify_shell: set -eo pipefail; cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta; MINIO_LEFT_OPS=$(grep -r -i 'minio' repos/ops 2>/dev/null | wc -l); MINIO_LEFT_NOETL=$(grep -r -i 'minio' repos/noetl/noetl 2>/dev/null | wc -l); HAS_SEAWEED=$(test -d repos/ops/ci/manifests/seaweedfs && echo 1 || echo 0); HAS_RUSTFS=$(test -d repos/ops/ci/manifests/rustfs && echo 1 || echo 0); HAS_OBJECT_STORE_PLAYBOOK=$(test -f repos/ops/automation/infrastructure/object_store.yaml && echo 1 || echo 0); MINIO_PLAYBOOK_GONE=$(test ! -f repos/ops/automation/infrastructure/minio.yaml && echo 1 || echo 0); echo "minio_in_ops:$MINIO_LEFT_OPS minio_in_noetl_code:$MINIO_LEFT_NOETL seaweedfs:$HAS_SEAWEED rustfs:$HAS_RUSTFS object_store_playbook:$HAS_OBJECT_STORE_PLAYBOOK minio_playbook_gone:$MINIO_PLAYBOOK_GONE"; if [ "$MINIO_LEFT_OPS" -le 0 ] && [ "$HAS_SEAWEED" -eq 1 ] && [ "$HAS_RUSTFS" -eq 1 ] && [ "$HAS_OBJECT_STORE_PLAYBOOK" -eq 1 ] && [ "$MINIO_PLAYBOOK_GONE" -eq 1 ]; then echo 'GOAL: MINIO_ELIMINATED_CHOOSER_LANDED'; else echo 'GOAL: NOT_YET'; fi

denied_tools: Modifying repos/gui or repos/e2e unless inventory phase 1 surfaces an unexpected MinIO touchpoint there
Keeping MinIO as a fallback option — user explicitly said 'eliminate MinIO completely'
Changing the actual S3-client code path in noetl (it's already backend-agnostic via NOETL_S3_ENDPOINT)
Cutting any release — let semantic-release handle versioning
Provisioning new GCP/AWS buckets — chooser is for in-cluster backends
preferred_tools: grep / grep -r for inventory and verification sweeps
noetl validate / Pydantic for playbook validation
cd repos/docs && npm install && npm run build
git apply / git commit -F - / git push (3 topic branches)
gh pr create / gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260511-150000-eliminate-minio-add-seaweedfs-rustfs-chooser.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_inventory_and_design_pick: {files_added_to_inventory, canonical_service_name, canonical_port, default_objectstore, image_tags}, 2_new_manifests: {seaweedfs_files, rustfs_files, dry_run_clean: bool}, 3_infrastructure_playbooks: {object_store_yaml_created: bool, minio_yaml_deleted: bool, bootstrap_updated: bool, destroy_updated: bool}, 4_helm_chart_chooser: {values_yaml_updated: bool, helm_template_both_backends_clean: bool}, 5_configmap_and_endpoint_wiring: {ops_configmaps_updated: bool, noetl_configmaps_updated: bool, endpoint_url_value}, 6_noetl_code_comment_sweep: {files_updated, tests_passing: bool}, 7_docs_sweep: {minio_refs_remaining: 0, docs_build_clean: bool}, 8_smoke_kind: {seaweedfs_pod_healthy: bool, rustfs_pod_healthy: bool, durability_test_seaweedfs: bool, durability_test_rustfs: bool}, 9_smoke_gke: {gke_s3_was, gke_now_using, durability_test: bool}, 10_open_and_merge_prs: {ops_pr, noetl_pr, docs_pr}, 11_close_out_and_pointer_bumps: {ops_sha, noetl_sha, docs_sha, ai_meta_commit_local, sync_issue_closed: bool, validation_log_appended: bool}}, regressions_found, iterations, verdict, notes, deferred_followups: ['Cloud-tier router decision (GCS vs S3 for cross-cluster/durable layer) — distinct from in-cluster object store', 'GKE remote-S3 path (if used) — clarify deployment model in docs', 'Ollama bridge deployment on GKE if real Ollama wanted', 'Amadeus production API switch']}. If verdict GREEN, append paragraph to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md.
